title: Rajasthan’s Churu Faces Extreme Heatwave as Temperatures Cross 44 Degrees - The CSR Journal
full_text:
The town of Churu in Rajasthan is currently grappling with an intense heatwave, with temperatures reportedly crossing the 44-degree mark. This extreme weather has significantly disrupted daily activities, leaving local markets nearly deserted as residents opt to remain indoors to escape the scorching heat.
Those who do venture outside are seen making efforts to hydrate themselves, often stopping for cold beverages to cope with the oppressive heat. The situation has led to complaints from residents about the diminishing facilities available to support them during such extreme weather.
Residents Share Their Experiences of the Heat
Local resident Nidhi Sharma expressed her concerns about the escalating temperatures, stating that the current conditions are extremely challenging. According to her, Churu is known for its harsh summer temperatures combined with the cold winters. She indicated that community members are mainly staying indoors and consuming refreshing drinks such as juices and buttermilk to alleviate the discomfort caused by the heat.
Another resident, Hanuman Sharma, highlighted the lack of public amenities available for cooling in the city. He described his experience after returning from outside, where he was drenched in sweat. Sharma noted that animals and birds are also struggling to cope with the weather. He lamented that there are insufficient public resources like water and fans, making it difficult for residents to find relief from the heat.
Nandita Sharma, who recently returned to her hometown from Kolkata, remarked on the severity of Churu’s heat. She referred to it as infamous and shared her decision to avoid dining out, opting instead for homemade cold drinks like buttermilk and lassi. Nandita explained that going to the market during the afternoon has become untenable due to the extreme hot winds that the region has been experiencing.
Weather Forecast Indicates Continued Heatwave
According to the India Meteorological Department (IMD), heatwave conditions are expected to persist in Rajasthan from May 24 to May 30. The IMD has also warned of severe heatwave conditions for isolated areas in West Rajasthan during this timeframe.
Furthermore, the weather department noted that heatwave phenomena have already been observed in various regions, including Vidarbha, Chhattisgarh, East Uttar Pradesh, Bihar, Madhya Pradesh, and Odisha. This broader trend highlights the extent of the current heatwave affecting multiple areas across northern and central India.
As residents in Churu continue to navigate the extreme heat, local authorities may need to consider measures to support public health and safety during this period. The combination of high temperatures and insufficient resources poses significant challenges for the community, prompting many to seek ways to cope with the severe weather conditions.

title: India News | Rajasthan: Churu Swelters Under Severe Heatwave as Mercury Crosses 44-degree Mark - LatestLY
full_text:
Churu (Rajasthan) [India], May 24 (ANI): Normal life has been thrown out of gear in Churu with a blistering heatwave, as the mercury has crossed the 44-degree mark, according to the regional weather department.
Local markets are nearly empty as residents remain confined to their homes to escape the scorching sun. Those venturing out for urgent work are seen seeking refuge in beverages to stay hydrated and combat the intense heat.
Speaking to ANI, residents shared their struggles with the rising temperatures.
Nidhi Sharma, a young resident, said, "The heat in Churu is rising day by day, and for the past few days, it's been excessively hot with scorching winds. Going out of the house has become very difficult. As everyone knows, Churu experiences extreme temperatures, both in summer and winter. In winter, it drops below zero, and in summer, it's incredibly hot. To cope with this, we mostly stay indoors and consume cold drinks like juices, buttermilk, and curd."
Hanuman Sharma, a resident, highlighted the lack of public facilities, saying, "Today is particularly intense; I just came from outside and am drenched in sweat. Even animals and birds are struggling. Unfortunately, there are no public facilities in the city to provide cooling or even water. Fans and coolers are failing to provide relief, and while we drink buttermilk and other cold liquids, they don't seem to help much."
Nandita Sharma, who visited her hometown from Kolkata, expressed the severity of the weather, saying, "What can I say about Churu's heat? It's famous. I came from Kolkata, but my hometown is Churu. The heat is beyond words. We've stopped eating out and are sticking to homemade drinks like buttermilk, lemon soda and lassi. Going to the market in the afternoon is out of the question because of the intense 'loo' (hot winds) we've been experiencing for the past few days,"
According to the IMD, heatwave conditions are very likely in Rajasthan from May 24 to May 30. Furthermore, severe heatwave conditions are predicted for isolated pockets over West Rajasthan during the same period.
The weather department also noted that heatwave conditions have already prevailed across many places in Vidarbha, Chhattisgarh, East Uttar Pradesh, Bihar, Madhya Pradesh, and Odisha. (ANI)
(The above story is verified and authored by ANI staff, ANI is South Asia's leading multimedia news agency with over 100 bureaus in India, South Asia and across the globe. ANI brings the latest news on Politics and Current Affairs in India & around the World, Sports, Health, Fitness, Entertainment, & News. The views appearing in the above post do not reflect the opinions of LatestLY)

title: Rajasthan: चूरू में भीषण लू का प्रकोप, पारा 44 डिग्री के पार - Janta Se Rishta
full_text:
Churu : क्षेत्रीय मौसम विभाग के अनुसार, चूरू में भीषण गर्मी की लहर के कारण सामान्य जनजीवन अस्त-व्यस्त हो गया है, क्योंकि पारा 44 डिग्री के आंकड़े को पार कर गया है। स्थानीय बाज़ार लगभग खाली पड़े हैं, क्योंकि लोग चिलचिलाती धूप से बचने के लिए अपने घरों में ही दुबके हुए हैं। जो लोग ज़रूरी काम से बाहर निकल रहे हैं, वे खुद को हाइड्रेटेड रखने और भीषण गर्मी का मुकाबला करने के लिए ठंडे पेय पदार्थों का सहारा लेते नज़र आ रहे हैं। ANI से बात करते हुए, लोगों ने बढ़ते तापमान के कारण होने वाली अपनी मुश्किलों को साझा किया।
निधि शर्मा, एक युवा निवासी, ने कहा, "चूरू में गर्मी दिन-ब-दिन बढ़ती जा रही है, और पिछले कुछ दिनों से, यह बहुत ज़्यादा गर्म है और साथ में लू भी चल रही है। घर से बाहर निकलना बहुत मुश्किल हो गया है। जैसा कि सभी जानते हैं, चूरू में गर्मी और सर्दी, दोनों मौसमों में तापमान बहुत ज़्यादा रहता है। सर्दियों में यह शून्य से नीचे चला जाता है, और गर्मियों में यह अविश्वसनीय रूप से गर्म हो जाता है। इससे निपटने के लिए, हम ज़्यादातर घर के अंदर ही रहते हैं और जूस, छाछ और दही जैसे ठंडे पेय पदार्थों का सेवन करते हैं।" हनुमान शर्मा, एक निवासी, ने सार्वजनिक सुविधाओं की कमी पर प्रकाश डालते हुए कहा, "आज गर्मी विशेष रूप से भीषण है; मैं अभी-अभी बाहर से आया हूँ और पसीने से तरबतर हूँ। यहाँ तक कि जानवर और पक्षी भी परेशान हैं। दुर्भाग्य से, शहर में ठंडक या पानी उपलब्ध कराने के लिए कोई सार्वजनिक सुविधाएँ नहीं हैं। पंखे और कूलर भी राहत देने में नाकाम साबित हो रहे हैं, और हालाँकि हम छाछ और अन्य ठंडे तरल पदार्थ पी रहे हैं, लेकिन उनसे भी ज़्यादा मदद मिलती नहीं दिख रही है।"
नंदिता शर्मा, जो कोलकाता से अपने गृहनगर आई थीं, ने मौसम की गंभीरता को व्यक्त करते हुए कहा, "मैं चूरू की गर्मी के बारे में क्या कहूँ? यह तो मशहूर है। मैं कोलकाता से आई हूँ, लेकिन मेरा गृहनगर चूरू ही है। गर्मी इतनी ज़्यादा है कि शब्दों में बयाँ नहीं की जा सकती। हमने बाहर खाना-पीना बंद कर दिया है और घर पर बने पेय पदार्थों जैसे छाछ, नींबू सोडा और लस्सी पर ही निर्भर हैं। दोपहर में बाज़ार जाना तो बिल्कुल ही मुमकिन नहीं है, क्योंकि पिछले कुछ दिनों से हम भीषण 'लू' (गर्म हवाओं) का सामना कर रहे हैं।"
IMD के अनुसार, राजस्थान में 24 मई से 30 मई तक गर्मी की लहर (हीटवेव) की स्थिति बने रहने की बहुत ज़्यादा संभावना है। इसके अलावा, इसी अवधि के दौरान पश्चिमी राजस्थान के कुछ अलग-अलग इलाकों में भीषण गर्मी की लहर की स्थिति का भी पूर्वानुमान है। मौसम विभाग ने यह भी बताया कि विदर्भ, छत्तीसगढ़, पूर्वी उत्तर प्रदेश, बिहार, मध्य प्रदेश और ओडिशा के कई स्थानों पर पहले से ही लू की स्थिति बनी हुई है।
